reasoning: The option-adjusted spread (BAMLEMHBHYCRPIOAS) hovered around 3.26–3.35 for most of February 2026 before spiking to 3.44 on Saturday, February 28. As the index notes state, weekend observations only occur as month-end accrued interest adjustments, so March 1 (Sunday) will not have an observation and its benchmark value will carry forward the 3.44 print. This 3.44 level is unusually elevated compared to recent weeks, having jumped 18 basis points over just three trading days. Without a confirmed structural credit event in emerging markets—news searches show a generally stable/positive outlook for EM debt relative to US high yield—this rapid increase is likely either a transient market shock or technical month-end volatility, making mean reversion the most probable short-term outcome. The linear auto-regression expects a value near 3.36 for early March. Thus, the probability of strictly exceeding the elevated 3.44 baseline on March 8 is relatively low (approx. 32%). For the March 31 target, the forecast horizon is longer, allowing more potential for macroeconomic shifts, broader spread widening, or another month-end technical adjustment, pushing the probability of increase slightly closer to 50% (approx. 42%).
